$comment: Registry of deliberately forked gate scripts. Each consuming skill carries its own byte-identical copy because ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} is the only portable path available to skill Bash calls. Duplication is intentional; silent drift is the bug. Never hand-edit a copy: edit the canonical, then run `python3 scripts/fork_sync.py --write`. To add a consumer skill, append its copy path here and run --write. check.py (§5) and supply_chain_check.py assert byte-identity from this file in CI.

forks.why: architecture package integrity and stale-source gate: /core-engineering:ce-architecture validates before publishing; /core-engineering:ce-spec and /core-engineering:ce-implement validate a package before design or code mutation; and /core-engineering:ce-auto-build validates a required current baseline before spawning. Each consumer bundles its own copy because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only portable path available to skill Bash calls.
spec hard-gate (H1-H4) runs in interactive /core-engineering:ce-spec and in the auto-build orchestrator
test gate runs per-task in interactive implement (snapshot mode) and per-feature in the auto-build orchestrator
Scope Lock file-boundary gate: runs in-loop in interactive /core-engineering:ce-implement (Stage 2) and per-feature in the auto-build orchestrator; also the merge-bar's implement-scope advisory gate
dependency gate: per-task in implement, per-feature in auto-build, cross-feature dependency-manifest backstop in verify
typosquat reference data consumed by dep-guard.py — must ship beside every dep-guard copy

evidence-stamp writer + freshness `check`: `/core-engineering:ce-implement` stamps done-ness, while `/core-engineering:ce-verify` and `/core-engineering:ce-ship-release` each run `check` to downgrade a task to `stale` when its commit_sha left HEAD's ancestry. Each consumer bundles its own copy because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash

evidence compiler: /core-engineering:ce-retro runs it as its aggregation floor, and /core-engineering:ce-ship-release's evidence-pack invokes it as a same-directory sibling subprocess (embedding its JSON), so the pack script needs a byte-identical copy beside it — CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash

auditor-consumable evidence pack composer: /core-engineering:ce-retro exports it and /core-engineering:ce-ship-release generates a per-release pack; each bundles its own copy (with its own audit-export.py sibling) because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash

session write lease set/restore for every read-only-on-code skill — the Stage-0/exit halves of the write-scope-guard lease discipline; each skill bundles its own copy because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash

review-summary schema and blocking gate: /core-engineering:ce-review owns the artifact contract and /core-engineering:ce-auto-build validates each worker result in-loop; the orchestrator bundles a synchronized copy because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only portable path available to skill Bash calls

plan structural-integrity lint (H1-H10 + advisories), including the plan-root architecture-direction artifact binding: /core-engineering:ce-plan-audit runs it as its hard-lint floor; /core-engineering:ce-plan runs it as a Stage-9 write-time gate; /core-engineering:ce-spec and /core-engineering:ce-implement validate the resolved plan before consuming its architecture posture; and /core-engineering:ce-auto-build plan-lints at Stage-0 kickoff before any spawn. Each consumer bundles its own copy plus the colocated architecture-selection-lint.py dependency because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash calls. Also the merge-bar's plan-lint advisory gate.

architecture-direction selection integrity and freshness gate: /core-engineering:ce-plan validates transient exploration results and the final plan-root architecture-selection.json; plan-lint dynamically loads the colocated validator in /core-engineering:ce-plan-audit, /core-engineering:ce-architecture, /core-engineering:ce-spec, /core-engineering:ce-implement, and /core-engineering:ce-auto-build. Each consumer bundles a byte-identical copy because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash calls.

Brief-to-plan skip authority is hash- and content-bound: /core-engineering:ce-brief validates before handoff, and /core-engineering:ce-plan revalidates the exact pair before skipping any Stage 1.4 question. The plan consumer uses --skip-persona-check because persona-library resolution is producer validation; hash, schema, complete section coverage, and answered-content checks remain hard.

the shared merge-bar finding-disposition ledger reader. Two advisory gates consume it in-place from their own skill dirs — secrets-guard.py (ce-probe-infra, canonical beside it) and sca-guard.py (ce-probe-deps) — and each imports it dir-locally because CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR is the only path guarantee in skill Bash and a merge-bar gate must resolve its imports beside itself. disposition-lint.py (ce-probe-infra) imports the canonical. evidence-pack.py is a third kind of reader — a REPORTER, not a gate: it renders the ledger as the pack's accepted-risk register (active vs expired, never suppressed), so a co-shipped sibling ships beside each of ITS two copies (ce-retro canonical + ce-ship-release fork); portability_check.py's nonstdlib_imports() allows a sibling `<name>.py` import for exactly this. One schema, no share-by-reference; edit the canonical and fork_sync.
